page: Microsoft Azure F64-16ams_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Famsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The processor includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache, with support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI. This instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is capable of utilizing external Premium Storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression both placing in the top 10% tier. Multi-threaded decompression via bzip3 is also notable, ranking in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and overall PassMark CPU scores fall within the average tier. Memory performance is robust, with a PassMark memory score in the top 25%, though uncached memory read and write bandwidths remain in the average tier. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 427.82 tokens per second on a small 135M parameter model, though performance scales down to average levels for larger 7B and 70B models.

The F64-16ams_v7 presents a specific resource density profile, offering a massive memory footprint relative to its 16 vCPUs. This high memory-to-CPU ratio makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require proportional scaling of compute cores, thereby avoiding the licensing or resource costs of over-provisioning vCPUs. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy operations. This server is suited for memory-intensive applications, database caching layers, large-scale data processing, and lightweight language model inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure F64-16ams_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the Famsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 16-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of RAM, providing an exceptional 32.0 GB of memory per core, but lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression, alongside strong memory performance and fast text generation for small-scale language models. However, multi-core compute and memory bandwidth metrics remain average. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads that do not require high vCPU counts, minimizing compute over-provisioning. It is suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and data processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F64-16ams_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring a 16-core AMD EPYC processor, 512 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, suited for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F64-16ams_v7 is a memory-optimized server with 16 AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: An AMD-powered server delivering an exceptional memory-to-core ratio and top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

512.0 GB of system memory providing 32.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage, requiring network-attached Premium Storage
